projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

needs_attention.title: Contact installers for quotes

observations.summary: Penelope is standing in the kitchen area, focused on a small object in her hands. In the background, a dog can be heard barking at the dog run.
The entryway is currently empty, with no people or animals visible in the frame. However, audio sensors detect movement and an impact sound occurring in this area.
Penelope is standing in the kitchen area, looking towards the living room. A dog can be heard barking at the dog run outside.
Penelope is walking through the living room and kitchen area while carrying a blue piece of clothing. She appears to be moving between different parts of the house.
The side passage and backyard area are empty and still. The white gate is closed, blocking the path toward the driveway.
Penelope is moving through the living room and kitchen area, bending down near the couch before standing back up.
The side passage and backyard area are currently empty and still. No people, dogs, or significant movement are observed in the visible area, despite some recorded impact/movement sounds.
No water flow. Daily usage: 670.5 gal.
The dog run is empty, with a tennis ball and a small red ball resting on the artificial grass. No dogs or people are present in the yard at this time.

The front yard camera is non-functional and is displaying an error message instead of video. While audio from the backyard and a dog barking in the living room are being captured, no visual information is available for the front yard area.

The front door camera is currently non-functional, displaying an error message indicating that no video frames are being received. While audio activity is detected in the backyard and dog run, the front entrance cannot be visually monitored.

insights.summary: [entryway] Audio indicates movement or an impact sound in the entryway, but no person or animal is visible on camera.

There is a significant discrepancy between the high volume of impact/movement and unclassified audio alerts (15:11 - 15:18) and the camera's visual confirmation, which reports the backyard, dog run, and entryway as empty and still. This suggests a sudden, localized event occurred that generated noise but was either too brief, too small, or occurred while the cameras were experiencing a temporary blind spot, warranting a manual check of the area.
Unusually quiet at whole_home this hour: 0 observations vs typical 2 for weekday 15:00
Pattern: dogs have been unusually active in the backyard on 3 of the last 7 days (avg 4 observations/day). This may indicate fence activity, a neighborhood animal, or family routine changes.
Dogs barking repeatedly at front yard (3x since 1:59PM).
Dogs barking repeatedly at living room (2x since 2:06PM).
[front_yard] The front yard camera is non-functional and is not transmitting any video frames.
[doorbell] The front door camera is offline, creating a blind spot at the main entrance.

There is a significant discrepancy between the camera reports (which repeatedly state the backyard is 'empty') and the audio logs (which show multiple instances of impact/movement sounds and dog barking between 14:11 and 14:17). This suggests that activity is occurring in the backyard that is not being captured by the cameras, creating a blind spot for monitoring, especially given the persistent technical failures of the front door and front yard cameras.
Dogs barking repeatedly at front yard (2x since 1:59PM).
